comment: Template configuration file - Copy to organizer_config.json and customize

drives.comment: Define drive shortcuts here. You can use these names in sync_pairs and source_folders below. If a drive is not available, the program will skip it gracefully.

sync_pairs.comment: Example: Using drive placeholders with cloud storage
Example: You can also use direct paths
Note: Folders A and B are interchangeable. Files sync bidirectionally based on:
sync_pairs.comment2: 1. If file exists in A but not B → copy from A to B
sync_pairs.comment3: 2. If file exists in B but not A → copy from B to A
sync_pairs.comment4: 3. If file exists in both but A is newer → copy from A to B
sync_pairs.comment5: 4. If file exists in both but B is newer → copy from B to A

comment_source_folders: Optional: roots to scan for duplicate detection when enable_duplicate_detection is true. You can use drive placeholders here (e.g. MAIN_DRIVE/Documents).
comment_softlink: Folders matching these patterns are backed up to ~/organised (maintaining full source path structure) and replaced with soft links. This prevents their contents from being copied during sync operations.
comment_empty: Folders matching these patterns have their contents deleted (leaving empty folders) every time file_organizer.py finds them.
comment_exclude: Files matching these patterns are excluded from sync. Note: ~/organised is always automatically excluded.
comment_output: Where organized files are stored (with soft links). Use ~ for home directory.
comment_sync: enable_folder_sync: true = sync folders, false = only organize files
comment_rsync_mode: checksum = thorough but SLOW. timestamp = fast (recommended)
comment_chunking: Large folders sync in chunks. Each chunk times out after 60 min to prevent infinite hangs.
comment_soft_links: Limit how many categories a single file can be linked to. Prevents over-categorization.

ml_content_analysis.comment: min_word_length: 5 avoids short common words. Higher max_categories discovers more topics. use_clip: false by default (very slow on CPU, ~30min first load)
